# fmc_intrusion_rules (Resource)

This resource manages Intrusion Rules through bulk operations.

The following restrictions apply:
  - Bulk object deletion is not supported by FMC, it will be handled one-by-one
  - Updates are always done one-by-one.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "fmc_intrusion_rules" "example" {
  items = {
    my_intrusion_rules = {
      rule_data = "alert icmp any any -> any any ( sid:10000301; gid:2000; msg:\"CUSTOM RULE1\"; classtype:icmp-event; rev:1; )"
      rule_groups = [
        {
          id   = "12da34567890-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678"
          name = "Custom Rule Group 1"
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `items` (Attributes Map) 'Map of Intrusion Rules. The key of the map is the name of the individual Intrusion Rule. Use the name in gid:sid format (eg. 2000:10000301).' (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--items))

### Optional

- `domain` (String) Name of the FMC domain

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Id of the object

<a id="nestedatt--items"></a>
### Nested Schema for `items`

Required:

- `rule_data` (String) Snort formated rule data.
- `rule_groups` (Attributes List) List of Intrusion Rule Groups this rule belongs to. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--items--rule_groups))

Read-Only:

- `id` (String) Id of the Intrusion Rule object.
- `type` (String) Type of the object; this value is always 'IntrusionRule'.

<a id="nestedatt--items--rule_groups"></a>
### Nested Schema for `items.rule_groups`

Required:

- `id` (String) Id of the Intrusion Rule Group.

Optional:

- `name` (String) Name of the Intrusion Rule Group.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# <domain> is optional. If not provided, `Global` is used implicitly and resource's `domain` attribute is not set.
terraform import fmc_intrusion_rules.example "<domain>,[<item1_name>,<item2_name>,...]"
```
